reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes the informal claim. The hypothesis `p.Prime ∧ p > 2` is equivalent to "p is an odd prime" since 2 is the only even prime. Setting `f = (X+2)^p`, all binomial coefficients `C(p,k) * 2^(p-k)` are positive, so `f.support = {0,1,...,p}` and `f.support \ {0} = {1,...,p}`. The sum `∑ n ∈ f.support \ {0}, f.coeff n * X^(n-1)` therefore equals `∑_{k=1}^p C(p,k) 2^(p-k) X^(k-1)`, which is exactly `((X+2)^p - 2^p)/X` — removing the constant term 2^p and shifting down by X. Since n ≥ 1 in all summands, ℕ-subtraction `n-1` is well-defined. `Irreducible` in `Polynomial ℤ` matches irreducibility in ℤ[x]. No hypotheses are added or dropped relative to the informal statement.
